reason: Fully cooked shelf-stable cakes, biscuits and pastries without meat are allowed for personal consumption.
Cheese, butter and dairy products must be declared and inspected on arrival.
Chocolate and confectionery are generally allowed for personal consumption.
CRoasted coffee is permitted into Australia for personal use if it is roasted, ground or processed into instant coffee.
Kopi Luwak/Civet coffee must be declared and inspected on arrival.
Green coffee is allowed into Australia, however the goods must be accompanied by a phytosanitary certificate.
Dried herbs and loose herbal teas must be declared and may be inspected for biosecurity risks.
Fish products must be declared and may be inspected to confirm they meet import conditions.
Salmon and trout products must be declared and inspected due to biosecurity risk.
Food taken from aircraft or ships cannot be brought into Australia.
Honey products must be declared and inspected to ensure they are free from contamination.
Breast milk must be declared and may only be imported in limited quantities for infant use.
Instant beverage sachets containing dairy must be declared and may be inspected.
Infant formula must be declared and must meet specific country-of-origin requirements.
Juice and soft drinks must be declared to confirm they are commercially packaged.
Maple syrup is allowed into Australia for personal use.
Meat products have strict biosecurity controls and must always be declared.
Noodles or pasta must be declared to verify ingredients and packaging.
Nuts must be declared and may be inspected to ensure they meet processing requirements.
Vegetable and seed oils are allowed if free from contamination.
Pepper must be declared and labelled with the botanical name of the permitted species.
Pet food requires an import permit or documentation and must be declared.
Prawns and prawn products must be declared and may require specific permits.
Preserved fruit and vegetables must be declared to confirm acceptable preservation methods.
Raw (uncooked) rice is not permitted and will be destroyed.
Rice must be declared due to potential biosecurity risks.
Sauces and curry pastes must be declared and confirmed to be shelf stable and commercially packaged.
Seafood, shellfish and snails must be declared and inspected for biosecurity compliance.
Spices must be declared and inspected to ensure they are free from pests and contaminants.
Tea products must be declared and may be inspected for contamination.
Tea products must be declared and may be inspected for contamination.
Vitamins and supplements must be declared and limited to personal use quantities.
Wheat must be declared and is only allowed if commercially milled.
Whole eggs require an import permit and must be declared.
